- Villain VPIP 83%, PFR 17% — very loose preflop caller, but passive aggressor
- fold_to_bet: 5/5 opportunities (83%+ fold to bets postflop) — extremely exploitable
- With **Ad Tc** on the button (SB), I have a strong hand against a loose-passive villain
- Raising preflop gains initiative for profitable c-bets given villain's high fold-to-bet tendency

**Decision:** Raise to 6 (3x BB) — standard opening raise, sets up c-bet pressure on flop where villain folds heavily.

Board: T J 9 rainbow. I have K6cc - I have a gutshot straight draw (Q makes the broadway straight... wait: 9-T-J board, I need Q for QJT9 or 8 for 9T... actually I need Q for QJKT or 8 for 8-9-T-J. Wait: 9,T,J on board. Straight draws: 7-8 makes 7-8-9-T-J, 8-Q makes 8-9-T-J-Q, Q-K makes Q-K... no. With K in hand: K-Q would give Q-K but need A for broadway. 


So really I'm looking at K as an overcard and a backdoor club flush draw since the board is all diamonds, hearts, and spades. My opponent played loose preflop and just bet 12 into 28 on this coordinated board - they could easily have hands like QT, KQ, or 89 that connect well here. This is a weak spot for me, so folding makes sense.
